divergence: The sun was privatized and daylight became a premium tier
story_angle: deadpan report of a catastrophe that everyone involved considers a great success
headline: Great Nocturnal Famine Declared a Rousing Success: Lords Rejoice as Daylight Becomes Luxury Good
deck: With serfs nibbling on rivet shavings, Copper Synod celebrates exemplary market discipline in darkness.
article:
In a historic proclamation, the Lords of the Eclipse Consortium have resolutely declared the Great Nocturnal Famine a triumph of privatized solar management, reporting a staggering 14,872 copper sovereigns amassed from premium dusk tariffs during the seven-day blackout. While the serfs of the brass-terraced hamlets now subsist on boiled rivet shavings, the Copper Synod, with their perfectly symmetrical faces, remained unfazed, attributing this unfortunate malady to what they have dubbed 'exemplary market discipline.'

Lord Methuselah-19, in a grand display of bureaucratic audacity, has been publicly noted as having breached solar edicts yet retains all eclipse licenses following the affixing of a royal copper monitoring tag. 'It is but a minor oversight,' he stated, adjusting his custom brass monocle, 'as our innovative system ensures every lord's right to profit remains intact, even amidst the pitch darkness enveloping peasant life.'

Just last week, the summons for 'Dawn Demand'—the official application form required for any unlicensed light—resulted in an unprecedented outpouring of peasant petitions, all pleading for a mere breath of the sun. However, clerical reports indicate that the standard processing time for such requests now exceeds the serfs' life expectancy, a fact the lords find both humorous and gratifying.

As the echoes of hunger ring hollow across Jovion, the Copper Synod appears undeterred, steadfast in their commitment to uphold the gilded mantle of the market while the sun's rays become but a mere whisper of a fading privilege.
author: Ezekiel-62, Royal Chronicler of the Lords' Enlightened Governance

weather.condition: Cloudy with a chance of paywalls

oped.title: Nocturnal Famine: Proof That Serfs Simply Lack Ambition
oped.author: Viscount Eldritch Candlewick, Certified Solar Apologist and Keeper of the Royal Rivet Shavings
oped.body: The Copper Synod’s triumph in privatizing the sun proves once more that markets reward virtue while punishing the hat-wearing rabble. Serfs complain of darkness yet refuse to purchase even the cheapest dusk tokens, preferring instead to nibble rivets like common rodents. Their oversized peasant caps, clearly designed to hoard stray photons, are the true villains here. Had they invested in premium daylight subscriptions rather than breeding, they might bask in the lords’ reflected glory. I myself paid handsomely for a personal noon sliver and resent any implication that shared gloom would suit my complexion. The obvious remedy is to privatize starlight next; only then will the ungrateful masses learn gratitude. Until that glorious day, let them chew metal in virtuous shadow.

classifieds.text: For sale: half a tallow taper that once saw a noon. Owner now dines on rivet shavings and will part with it for any scrap of premium daylight.
Help wanted: night-blind reeve seeks two stout lads to stand watch over the baron's remaining hour of sun. Wages paid in extra darkness and one boiled rivet.
Lost: a single beam of dawn stolen from my window during the Synod's celebration. Return it before matins and receive half a wheel of shadow-cheese.
Public notice: the undersigned lord hereby confesses he sold his last sunrise to the Copper Synod and now begs any passing serf for a candle stub on credit.

panels.caption: Serf scraping a rivet in blackness: "Best famine yet—the shavings have real bite."
Copper Synod clerk stamps a ledger: "Market discipline holds. Daylight futures cleared at triple last quarter's price."
Lord to clerk: "Then bill the serfs for the darkness they keep wasting."

joke.setup: A villein asked why the sun no longer rose for those without coin.
joke.punchline: The reeve answered, 'Because even God learned to charge the rabble for His light.'
joke.text: A villein asked why the sun no longer rose for those without coin. The reeve answered, 'Because even God learned to charge the rabble for His light.'

sponsor_ads.headline: Lumen Leases at the Royal Exchequer
sponsor_ads.body: Lease measured shafts of privatized sunlight for thy hall at three silver marks the hour, lest thy crops wither under the famine's profitable dark.

sponsor_ads.headline: Moon-Cured Shadowbread for the Successful Night
sponsor_ads.body: Baked solely in the Great Nocturnal Famine's approved gloom, this loaf sustains serfs who cannot afford daylight and prevents unseemly grumbling.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Lords exempt; may cause eternal dusk.

sponsor_ads.headline: Nightsoil to Daylight Transmutation
sponsor_ads.body: Our guild converts peasant waste into premium solar hours for noble purchase, turning the famine's triumph into coin for those who already own the sun.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Soul not included in first purchase.

sponsor_ads.headline: Darkness Bonds: The Only Commodity Still Free
sponsor_ads.body: Subscribe to unlit hours untouched by lordly rays; perfect for those priced out of noon and weary of begging borrowed beams from the consortium.

stories.headline: Rivet Shavings Declared New Staple as Nocturnal Famine Enters Second Week
stories.deck: Ezekiel29 reports three-acre plots now yielding only brass filings under eclipse licenses
stories.article:
Tenant holders on the copper terraces of Gilead12 have abandoned barley rows for the gutters beneath lordly awnings, where nightly collections of loosened rivets provide the sole caloric intake. Market ledgers record a fortyfold rise in filings traded against remaining eclipse tokens.

The Copper Synod has issued a new tithe schedule requiring one quarter of all shavings to be rendered to the vault at Bethel3 before dawn tokens expire.

stories.headline: Temple Lamps Extinguished After Final Oil Ration: Congregants Offered Shares in Remaining Eclipse Bonds
stories.deck: Priests of Isaiah44 announce that intercessions now require verified holdings in the Solar Trust
stories.article:
The copper-clad nave of Zion7 stands unlit for the first time since its founding, the last cask of lamp oil having been auctioned at midday to a consortium of Enoch31’s heirs. Worshippers are directed to recite the evening psalms in total darkness while clutching their bond certificates.

A decree from the Synod requires proof of premium subscription before any petition for restored dawn may be presented at the altar.

stories.headline: Night Watch Reports Rise in Unauthorized Light Siphoning from Lordly Terraces
stories.deck: Three serfs detained with smuggled vials of premium dusk at the gates of Jericho6
stories.article:
Constables of the Copper Watch have increased patrols along the riveted alleys of Manasseh4 following complaints that small quantities of retained daylight are being drained from private balconies after curfew. Offenders face immediate forfeiture of all eclipse tokens.

The magistrate at Solomon22 has ordered that any captured light be poured back into the Synod’s central reservoir before the next billing cycle closes.

real_world_echo: Marine Le Pen found guilty but cleared for presidential run if tagged - mirrored as Lord Methuselah-19 found in breach of solar edict yet permitted to retain all eclipse licenses upon affixing a royal copper monitoring tag
